words.simpleMeaning: to present yourself or someone else by name

sentences.office: He introduced the new manager to the team.
sentences.interview: I would like to introduce myself first.
sentences.business: Allow me to introduce our new product.
sentences.formal: The host introduced the guest speaker.
sentences.informal: Hey, let me introduce you to my friend.

words.simpleMeaning: to be from or a member of a place/group

sentences.office: She belongs to the sales team.
sentences.interview: I belong to a middle-class family.
sentences.formal: He belongs to a respected family.
sentences.informal: I belong here, this is my hometown.

words.usageNote: 'belong to' के बाद 's' नहीं लगाते — 'I belongs' गलत है, 'I belong' सही है।

sentences.daily: My origin is a small village.
sentences.office: Please state your city of origin.
sentences.interview: My family origin is from Rajasthan.
sentences.formal: He traced his origin to a royal family.

words.simpleMeaning: a job that needs special training

sentences.office: What is your profession?
sentences.interview: I am a doctor by profession.
sentences.formal: He practices law as his profession.

sentences.office: She has five years of experience.
sentences.interview: I have relevant experience for this role.
sentences.business: Our team has vast industry experience.
sentences.informal: I've got good experience, trust me.

words.simpleMeaning: having strong feelings or enthusiasm about something

sentences.office: She is passionate about her job.
sentences.interview: I am passionate about learning new skills.
sentences.business: We are passionate about customer service.
sentences.formal: He is passionate about his research.
sentences.informal: I'm so passionate about cricket, man.

sentences.daily: I am currently working from home.
sentences.office: She is currently on leave.
sentences.interview: I am currently working at ABC company.
sentences.business: We are currently expanding our team.

words.usageNote: Adverb, sentence में कहीं भी flexible position में आ सकता है।

words.simpleMeaning: a feeling of happiness or satisfaction

sentences.daily: It's a pleasure to meet you.
sentences.office: It was a pleasure working with you.
sentences.interview: It's my pleasure to be here today.
sentences.business: We are pleased to serve you.
sentences.formal: It gives me great pleasure to introduce him.

words.usageNote: 'It's a pleasure' एक common polite phrase है।

sentences.office: My sibling also works here.
sentences.interview: I am the eldest among my siblings.
sentences.informal: My sibling is younger than me.

words.usageNote: Gender-neutral word for भाई-बहन दोनों के लिए।

words.simpleMeaning: the town where you were born or grew up

sentences.office: I visit my hometown every year.
sentences.interview: My hometown is a small city in Punjab.
sentences.formal: He returned to his hometown after retirement.
sentences.informal: My hometown's amazing, you should visit!

words.usageNote: एक word है, दो अलग words नहीं।

sentences.interview: I was born in Agra but raised in Delhi.
sentences.formal: He was raised with strong values.
sentences.informal: I was raised in a small town.

sentences.daily: I am employed at a bank.
sentences.office: She is employed as an analyst.
sentences.interview: I am currently employed at XYZ Corp.

words.simpleMeaning: a skill or achievement that makes you suitable for something

sentences.office: Please list your qualifications.
sentences.interview: My qualification matches your requirements.

sentences.office: Teamwork is our strength.
sentences.interview: My biggest strength is problem-solving.

words.simpleMeaning: a fault or something you are not good at

sentences.interview: My weakness is that I am a perfectionist.
sentences.informal: My weakness is I talk too much.

words.usageNote: Interview में honest पर positive spin देना ज़रूरी है।

sentences.interview: My proudest achievement is winning the award.

words.simpleMeaning: to have a strong desire to achieve something

sentences.daily: I aspire to become a manager.
sentences.interview: I aspire to grow within this company.
sentences.formal: He aspires to serve the public.
sentences.informal: I aspire to travel the world.

sentences.daily: My goal is to learn English fluently.
sentences.office: Our goal is 100% customer satisfaction.
sentences.interview: My career goal is to become a team lead.
sentences.formal: He set a clear goal for himself.

words.simpleMeaning: a person's education, family, and experience

sentences.daily: I come from a business background.
sentences.office: Tell me about your professional background.
sentences.interview: I have a technical background in engineering.

words.simpleMeaning: able to speak a language easily and well

sentences.daily: I want to become fluent in English.
sentences.office: She is fluent in three languages.
sentences.interview: I am fluent in both Hindi and English.
sentences.informal: I'm getting more fluent every day.

sentences.daily: I feel confident speaking English now.
sentences.interview: I am confident about my skills.
sentences.formal: He appeared confident during the speech.

sentences.office: We communicate through email.
sentences.interview: I can communicate effectively in English.

words.simpleMeaning: in a way that earns money

sentences.interview: I am gainfully employed at present.

words.usageNote: Formal/interview context में इस्तेमाल होता है।

sentences.interview: I currently reside in Bangalore.

sentences.formal: I made his acquaintance last year.
sentences.informal: He's an old acquaintance of mine.

sentences.office: My colleagues are very supportive.
sentences.interview: I work well with my colleagues.
sentences.informal: My colleague and I go out for lunch.

words.usageNote: Work relationship के लिए इस्तेमाल होता है।

sentences.interview: I enjoy networking at events.
sentences.informal: I love networking with new people.

words.simpleMeaning: the act of presenting yourself or someone

sentences.interview: Give a brief introduction about yourself.

words.simpleMeaning: the characteristics that make a person unique

sentences.interview: My personality suits a customer-facing role.
sentences.informal: You've got such a cool personality!

words.usageNote: अक्सर interview में पूछा जाता है।

sentences.interview: I have an outgoing and friendly personality.
sentences.formal: She is known to be outgoing.
sentences.informal: He's super outgoing, everyone loves him.

sentences.daily: I am a bit reserved with strangers.
sentences.formal: He is reserved but very talented.
sentences.informal: He's kind of reserved at first.

words.usageNote: Negative नहीं, बस personality trait है।

sentences.office: Punctuality is important at work.
sentences.interview: I am very punctual and disciplined.
sentences.formal: He is known for being punctual.

words.usageNote: Interview में एक common strength है।

words.simpleMeaning: committed to a task or purpose

sentences.daily: I am dedicated to my work.
sentences.interview: I am a dedicated and hardworking employee.
sentences.informal: She's really dedicated to her studies.

sentences.interview: I am a hardworking and sincere person.
sentences.formal: He is known for being hardworking.

sentences.daily: I am sincere towards my responsibilities.
sentences.interview: I am a sincere and honest worker.

words.simpleMeaning: a very short introduction about yourself

sentences.office: Prepare your elevator pitch for the event.
sentences.interview: Give me your elevator pitch.
sentences.formal: His elevator pitch impressed the investors.

words.simpleMeaning: to be made up of certain parts

sentences.daily: My family consists of four members.
sentences.formal: The team consists of ten members.

sentences.daily: I am the eldest in my family.
sentences.informal: I'm the eldest, so I take care of everyone.

words.usageNote: Family में सबसे बड़े के लिए, 'oldest' generic है।

words.simpleMeaning: a family with only parents and children

sentences.daily: I live in a nuclear family.
sentences.formal: Nuclear families are common in cities.

sentences.daily: I come from a joint family.
sentences.formal: Joint families were common in the past.

words.usageNote: Grandparents, uncles सब एक साथ रहते हैं।

words.simpleMeaning: a person others try to be like

sentences.daily: My father is my role model.
sentences.interview: My role model is my mother.
sentences.formal: He is a role model for many.

words.usageNote: अक्सर interview questions में आता है।

words.simpleMeaning: the area you are best at

sentences.interview: My strength area is time management.

sentences.daily: I am self-taught in graphic design.
sentences.interview: I am mostly self-taught in coding.

sentences.daily: I am highly motivated to learn.
sentences.interview: I am a self-motivated individual.

words.usageNote: 'self-motivated' interview में बहुत इस्तेमाल होता है।

sentences.office: Be proactive in solving problems.
sentences.interview: I am a proactive team player.

words.simpleMeaning: someone who works well in a group

sentences.interview: I consider myself a good team player.

words.simpleMeaning: able to change easily to new situations

sentences.interview: I am adaptable to any work environment.

words.simpleMeaning: to do more than one task at a time

sentences.interview: I can multitask effectively.
I believe discipline is my biggest strength.
I have great eagerness to learn.

sentences.office: What is your current designation?
sentences.interview: My designation is Senior Analyst.

words.simpleMeaning: a collection of your work samples

sentences.interview: Here is my portfolio for review.
I completed an internship at a startup.

words.simpleMeaning: someone new to the job market

sentences.interview: I am a fresher looking for my first job.

words.simpleMeaning: hoping to become something in future

sentences.interview: I am an aspiring data analyst.

words.simpleMeaning: belonging to a place by birth

sentences.daily: I am a native of Kerala.
sentences.formal: He is a native speaker of Tamil.

words.simpleMeaning: someone who has finished a degree

sentences.daily: I am a graduate in commerce.
sentences.interview: I graduated in 2022 from Delhi University.
sentences.formal: He is a graduate of IIT.

words.simpleMeaning: to focus on a particular area of study or work

sentences.interview: I specialize in digital marketing.

sentences.daily: I want to pursue a career in IT.
sentences.interview: I am pursuing an MBA currently.
